alerts.title: Israel strikes Iran after direct ballistic missile attack; ceasefire collapses
alerts.summary: Iran launched at least 4 ballistic missiles at northern Israel in response to Israeli strikes on Beirut's Ad-Dakhieh Hezbollah headquarters, per Rybar and Israeli media. Israel conducted retaliatory strikes on Iranian targets including petrochemical facilities. This marks the first direct Iran-Israel kinetic exchange since April ceasefire, signaling collapse of de-escalation efforts and entry into sustained direct warfare phase.

alerts.title: Iran uranium enrichment reaches 60% U-235; breakout window 1-2 weeks
alerts.summary: US intelligence and IAEA confirm Iranian uranium enrichment at 60% U-235 purity with estimated breakout time narrowed to 1-2 weeks, per global_metrics nuclear headlines. Simultaneous US draft IAEA resolution demands Iran disclose fate of bombed nuclear sites and uranium stocks. Kinetic escalation combined with accelerated nuclear timeline creates compounding strategic crisis.

alerts.title: Trump orders Netanyahu to cease Iran retaliation; directive ignored overnight
alerts.summary: Trump stated he would call Netanyahu and demand Israel not strike Iran in response to Iranian missile attack, per Rybar Telegram analysis (~125K views). Despite Trump's de-escalation messaging and claims war would 'soon end,' Israel conducted ~20 strikes on Iranian military targets overnight across Tehran, Tabriz, Isfahan, Qeredagh, and Kermanshah. Signals US-Israel alignment fracture and failed diplomatic constraint.

alerts.title: US domestic sentiment collapses to 66% negative as Iran escalation dominates
alerts.summary: Negative sentiment in US news increased to 66% with Trump dominating trending terms (67 mentions alongside 'clash' and 'strikes'). Word frequency analysis shows 'interview,' 'deal,' and 'election' rising sharply, reflecting public anxiety over escalation control versus Trump's diplomatic posturing. Sentiment swing indicates deep domestic political division over conflict posture and credibility.

alerts.title: US carrier strike groups maintain critical positioning amid direct Iran-Israel war
alerts.summary: USS Dwight D. Eisenhower (Carrier) remains deployed in Red Sea; USS Abraham Lincoln (Carrier) positioned in Arabian Sea; USS Gerald R. Ford (Carrier) in Mediterranean; three destroyers (Carney, Mason, Laboon) maintain Red Sea stations; USS Florida (Submarine) in Persian Gulf. No repositioning detected, but sustained presence in all three crisis zones signals readiness for rapid escalation or evacuation operations during active Iran-Israel hostilities.

analysis_note: Direct Iran-Israel kinetic conflict has begun with ballistic missile and retaliatory strike exchanges following Israeli attack on Beirut; nuclear breakout window critically compressed to 1-2 weeks; Trump de-escalation messaging ineffective; ceasefire formally collapsed.
